sections.title: What famous landmarks do you know?

activities.title: Interview and report
activities.subtitle: Weekend habits vs last weekend
activities.instruction: Ask your classmates: What do you often do at the weekend? What did you do last weekend?

columns.label: At the weekend
columns.example: I often play football near my home.

columns.label: Last weekend
columns.example: I played Chinese chess last Saturday.

activities.reportTemplate: John often … at the weekend. But last weekend, he …

activities.title: Let's learn
activities.subtitle: Landmarks photo show
sentences: Amy walked around the Palace Museum for hours.
Mike visited the Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macao Bridge.
Liu Jia's family travelled to Paris and visited the Eiffel Tower.
Oliver and his cousin watched a show at the Sydney Opera House.

activities.title: Choose and talk
activities.subtitle: Tell me about a great place
activities.instruction: Choose a place and talk with a partner about what you did there.

sampleDialogue.speaker: Student A
sampleDialogue.text: I visited a great place last Saturday.

sampleDialogue.speaker: Student A
sampleDialogue.text: I climbed Mount Huangshan. I watched the sunrise.

activities.title: List and talk
activities.subtitle: Who had a special trip?
activities.instruction: Talk in groups. Who had a special trip? Take notes and report back.

sampleDialogue.speaker: Student 1
sampleDialogue.text: Where did you go over the Labour Day holiday?

sampleDialogue.speaker: Student 2
sampleDialogue.text: Tianjin. I ate the famous Goubuli baozi. They were good! I also watched a xiangsheng show. It was very funny.

sampleDialogue.speaker: Student 3
sampleDialogue.text: I went to Dunhuang. I saw many beautiful wall paintings …

prompts: We think the trip to … was special because …

activities.title: Read and write
activities.subtitle: Jinggang Mountains travel reviews
activities.instruction: Read the travel website reviews. Then answer the questions and write your own review.
paragraphs: Tags: No.1 Can't Miss Attraction in Ji'an · Mountain Climbing · UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site

My trip to the Jinggangshan Revolution Museum was amazing! I walked around the museum for hours and saw many old books and photos. The volunteers told great stories about the Red Army. The stories were really inspiring. I learnt a lot. Do you want to learn more about the Red Army? You should go there!

This was a great place for a summer trip! We climbed the Jinggang Mountains and took many photos of the bamboo forest and beautiful waterfalls. We tried delicious pumpkin soup and red rice in a nice restaurant too. The Jinggang Mountains are only about 1.5 hours from the airport by bus. I want to go there again!
questions: What did you learn about the Jinggang Mountains?
What does the word "must-see" mean in Sun Lin's review?
Do you want to go to the Jinggang Mountains after reading the reviews? Why?
Write your own travel review: title, opening sentence, what you did/saw/ate, and a closing sentence.
